text: This 33-year-old patient was referred for bronchoscopy after a CAT scan showed endoluminal growth in the trachea.
At first, the right main bronchus is divided from the primary carina.
The area of the trachea with tumor involvement is carefully divided proximally and distally, leaving normal tracheal margin above and below.
Here, you can notice the resected tracheal specimen with area of tumor involvement.

During the reconstruction anastomosis, an AMBA disposable bronchoscope is placed in the left main bronchus to which an oxygen port is connected to maintain ventilation during the process of anastomosis.
Surveillance bronchoscopy on third postoperative day showed well-formed neocarina as seen in this video.
